# loom (full context)

loom is built for PR comment workflows in GitHub pull requests.

Primary use cases:

1. Enumerate unresolved review threads
2. Filter by path/author/severity/text
3. Leave top-level or inline PR comments when needed
4. Edit or delete mistaken comments without dropping to raw API calls
5. Reply with status/resolution comments
6. Resolve/unresolve threads
7. Merge the PR when review state is clean
8. Re-check unresolved count until zero

## Commands

### List threads

`loom list --pr <number> [flags]`

Flags:

- `--repo owner/repo` (optional, inferred from upstream git remote)
- `--state unresolved|resolved|all` (default: unresolved)
- `--path <substring>`
- `--author <login>`
- `--query <text>` (preferred)
- `--contains <text>` (deprecated alias)
- `--severity critical|major|minor`
- `--sort updated|created|path|line|author|severity` (default: updated)
- `--desc` (default: true)
- `--limit <n>`
- `--format auto|table|json|jsonl` (preferred)
- `--json` (deprecated alias for `--format json`)
- `--stats`

### Find comments by query

`loom find --pr <number> --query <text> [flags]`

`find` accepts all main filter/sort/output flags from `list`.

### Comment

Preferred explicit modes:

- `loom comment-top --pr <number> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`
- `loom comment-inline --pr <number> --path <file> --line <n> --side LEFT|RIGHT (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`
- `loom comment-file --pr <number> --path <file> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`

`loom comment --pr <number> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`

If `--body` and `--body-file` are omitted, stdin is used.

For inline review comments, add:

- `--path <file>` plus `--line <n> --side LEFT|RIGHT` for single-line comments
- `--path <file> --start-line <n> --start-side LEFT|RIGHT --line <n> --side LEFT|RIGHT` for range comments
- `--path <file> --subject file` for file-level comments

If `--commit` is omitted for an inline comment, loom uses the PR head SHA automatically.

### Edit

`loom edit --comment-id <database-id-or-url> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--type auto|review|top-level] [--json]`

If `--type` is omitted, loom tries review comments first and then top-level PR conversation comments.

### Delete

`loom delete --comment-id <database-id-or-url> [--repo owner/repo] [--type auto|review|top-level] [--json]`

If `--type` is omitted, loom tries review comments first and then top-level PR conversation comments.

### Merge

`loom merge --pr <number> [--repo owner/repo] [--method merge|squash|rebase] [--json]`

If `--method` is omitted, loom uses `squash`.

### Reply

`loom reply --pr <number> --comment-id <database-id-or-url> (--body <text> | --body-file <path>) [--repo owner/repo] [--json]`

If `--body` and `--body-file` are omitted, stdin is used.

### Resolve

`loom resolve --thread-id <PRRT_...> [--json]`

Or resolve by review comment:

`loom resolve --repo owner/repo --pr <number> --comment-id <database-id-or-url> [--json]`

### Unresolve

`loom unresolve --thread-id <PRRT_...> [--json]`

## Output conventions

- Human mode: tabular output for `list/find`; short status lines for action commands.
- JSON mode:
  - `list/find`: array of thread records
  - `comment`: object with action/pr/comment_id/url
  - `edit`: object with action/type/comment_id/url
  - `delete`: object with action/type/comment_id
  - `merge`: object with action/pr/method/merged/sha/message
  - `reply`: object with action/comment_id/reply_id/url
  - `resolve/unresolve`: object with action/thread_id

## Operational notes

- Requires authenticated `gh` session.
- Uses GitHub GraphQL for thread data and resolve mutations.
- Uses GitHub REST issue comments endpoint for top-level PR comments.
- Uses GitHub REST pull request review comments endpoint for inline/file/range comments.
- Uses GitHub REST issue and review comment endpoints for edit/delete auto-detection.
- Uses GitHub REST merge endpoint for PR merges.
- Uses GitHub REST endpoint for review comment replies.
- `--stats` writes to stderr so stdout stays machine-readable with `--format json` or `--format jsonl`.
